title: Rockets and drones fly across Iran, Israel; Middle East remains tense — how the war is unfolding
content: Escalating Middle East conflict sees Israel strike Lebanon and intercept Iranian missiles. A US submarine sank an Iranian warship, while Iran retaliated with strikes across the region. The US Senate backed President Trump's military actions against Iran. Energy supplies are threatened as oil prices soar and global markets react.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 rises above 24,600; BSE Sensex up over 500 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex staged a comeback on Thursday after deep cuts in its last two trading sessions. While Nifty50 rose above 24,600, BSE Sensex was up over 500 points.

title: 'There's a little window to attack Samson': Faf gives Archer the blueprint
content: Sanju Samson delivered a match-winning 97 against the West Indies. This performance marks a significant turnaround for the Indian opener. However, former South Africa captain Faf du Plessis points to a vulnerability. High pace short balls above 140 kmph could trouble Samson. England may use this blueprint in their upcoming semifinal clash.

title: AWS datacentre fire update: Amazon urges Middle East customers to take action
content: Missile and drone attacks have severely impacted Amazon Web Services (AWS) data centers in the UAE and Bahrain, prompting urgent calls for customers to migrate operations. Major tech providers like Snowflake and Red Hat, also reliant on AWS, are advising Middle East clients to enact disaster recovery plans. Services are experiencing disruptions, with no immediate restoration ETA.

title: Ex-techie dies by suicide after quarrel with mother-in-law in Bengaluru; kin alleges harassment
content: A 35-year-old former software engineer, P Sushma, died by suicide in Bengaluru. Her father alleges dowry harassment and domestic fights by her husband, Puneet Kumar, and his mother. The couple married five years ago and has a four-year-old son. Police have arrested Puneet Kumar on charges of dowry death. Sushma's family claims they provided substantial dowry at the wedding.

title: From protests to polls: Nepal to vote after a Gen Z uprising redraws its political map
content: Nepal is at a critical juncture as nearly 19 million voters elect a new House of Representatives. This election is significantly influenced by a youth-led uprising that challenged the political establishment, fueled by resentment over unemployment and perceived elite excess. The vote reflects a generational demand for accountability and change.

title: Vijay Deverakonda's fun moment with paps goes viral; couple praised for style
content: Newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda hosted a grand reception, drawing a star-studded crowd. A playful moment saw Vijay humorously directing a photographer, which quickly went viral. Both stars looked resplendent in traditional attire, with Rashmika in a red Mysore silk saree and Vijay in a classic veshti and kurta. Fans eagerly await their on-screen reunion in 'Ranabaali'.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
content: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.

title: Top stocks to buy or sell: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Solar Industries India, and Coal India are the stocks that Aakash K Hindocha, Deputy Vice President - WM Research of Nuvama Professional Clients Group has recommended to buy today. UNO Minda is a sell call. The market expert shares his detailed analysis on stocks and views on Nifty and Bank Nifty.

title: T20 World Cup 2026: India, England ready for shootout at Wankhede
content: Heat, dew and high stakes set the stage at Wankhede as India and England gear up for a semi-final shootout. With Varun Chakravarthy’s rhythm under focus and Morkel backing instinct over caution, the clash could hinge on small margins and who reads conditions best.

title: Tariff blow for Trump admin: US court says firms must be refunded after court struck down levy
content: A New York judge has ordered the US government to refund companies for tariffs struck down by the Supreme Court. The ruling mandates refunds for duties imposed under a law the court deemed unconstitutional, impacting billions in collected taxes. This follows a Supreme Court decision limiting presidential tariff authority, with attention now on customs agencies to manage the mass payouts.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: Splashed with colours on Holi, woman hurls boiling water on 4-year-old in Nagpur
content: A shocking Holi incident in Nagpur saw a 60-year-old woman, Sindhu Thakre, scald a four-year-old boy with boiling water after he playfully sprayed colour on her. The child, Om Vange, is hospitalized with significant burns. Police have initiated legal action, emphasizing that such reckless acts, even if provoked, will not be tolerated, and are pushing for maximum charges.

title: Maharashtra transporters strike: Trucks, buses, taxis to go off roads from Friday
content: Transporters across Maharashtra have announced an indefinite strike starting Thursday midnight, protesting the alleged misuse of the e-challan system and arbitrary penalties. Talks with the transport commissioner failed, leading to the escalation of the protest, which has garnered support from private car owners and school bus operators. The strike aims to address issues like harassment by officers and unjustified fines.

title: 'Time to celebrate a little, but eyes on Sunday's final': Finn Allen after T20 World Cup century
content: Finn Allen's blistering 100 off 33 balls, the fastest T20 World Cup century ever, propelled New Zealand to a dominant nine-wicket victory over South Africa. Chasing 170, Allen and Tim Seifert's explosive opening partnership secured their spot in the final. New Zealand now awaits their opponent in Ahmedabad.

title: Middle East crisis: Trump rates US war effort ‘15 out of 10’
content: US President Trump declared the campaign against Iran "about a 15" on a scale of 10, citing success in decimating Iranian leadership and missile capabilities. He reiterated the goal of preventing nuclear weapons, vowing to continue joint operations with Israel despite escalating regional conflict, rising oil prices, and domestic criticism.

title: Nepal votes today: Pitted against Balen, Oli faces litmus test of 50-year-old career
content: Nepal's upcoming election sees a generational clash as Gen Z voters challenge traditional leadership. Despite facing constitutional crises and internal party dissent, veteran politician KP Sharma Oli, a former dissident who spent years in prison, remains a formidable force. He is now seeking re-election from his long-held constituency, facing a direct challenge from a younger contender.

title: 'They cannot come to our level': Kaif fires back at Amir in explosive rant
content: Mohammad Kaif sharply rebuked Mohammad Amir for his persistent doubts about India's T20 World Cup campaign. Kaif accused Amir of seeking publicity and highlighted Pakistan's loss to the USA, questioning Amir's credibility to critique others. He urged India to ignore such noise, emphasizing that engaging only amplifies the desired attention.

title: India’s biggest weakness exposed? Ex-India keeper raises red flag before England clash
content: Former India wicketkeeper Parthiv Patel expressed concern over the team's fielding standards ahead of their T20 World Cup semifinal against England. Despite reaching the last four, India's catching efficiency is the lowest in the tournament. Patel praised Sanju Samson's match-winning knock and Jasprit Bumrah's crucial wickets, emphasizing the need for early breakthroughs against England's strong batting lineup.

title: ‘A day of hope, peace and unity’: Donald & Melania Trump extend Holi greetings
content: US President Donald Trump extended warm greetings for Holi, recognizing the Hindu festival as a harbinger of spring and a symbol of good triumphing over evil. He conveyed wishes for hope, peace, and unity to all celebrating this vibrant festival of colours. The message underscores Holi's significance in fostering renewal and strengthening community bonds, particularly for the Indian diaspora.

title: Stocks empty or strategic move? Iran firing fewer missiles, says top US General
content: Iran's missile and drone attacks have significantly decreased, according to US military chief General Dan Caine.  Despite this, Washington is expanding its offensive deeper into Iranian territory.  Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared the US is winning, highlighting a submarine sinking an Iranian warship. The conflict has widened regionally, causing displacement and economic shockwaves.

title: PIO diplomat criticises UK foreign office for being woke, quits
content: A senior British diplomat of Indian origin has resigned, citing the Foreign Office's 'woke' culture and an overemphasis on international law at the expense of national security. Ameer Kotecha criticized the handling of issues like the Chagos Islands and Iran, arguing for a more Britain-first approach. He described the civil service as inefficient and out of touch with national priorities.

title: Cops book 8 Class X students under Pocso for sexual assault of younger boys at govt hostel
content: Eight Class X students at a Nashik government hostel are facing charges under the Pocso Act for allegedly sexually assaulting younger students for months. The abuse came to light when victims informed the hostel superintendent, who then contacted parents.

title: Shah Rukh Khan's role in Rajinikanth's 'Jailer 2' confirmed
content: Jailer 2 is taking the entertainment world by storm, with reports hinting that the iconic Shah Rukh Khan might be joining forces with the legendary Rajinikanth. A mysterious social media update from a costume designer, paired with Jatin Sarna's announcement about wrapping up his part—mentioning Vidya Balan and Mithun Chakraborty—has ignited a flurry of speculation.

title: Middle East tensions: Oil companies may cushion impact of spike - report
content: Oil marketing companies are absorbing recent crude oil price spikes, creating a buffer against inflation and GDP impacts. Despite a 16.8% rise in Brent crude, a full pass-through to consumers is unlikely, with OMCs expected to manage the increase through their margins. This strategy helps cushion India's economy from geopolitical shocks affecting its heavy reliance on imported oil.

title: Oxford museum set to return 500-year-old bronze statue taken from Tamil Nadu temple
content: A 16th-century bronze statue of Saint Tirumankai Alvar, taken from a Tamil Nadu temple, is returning to India from the UK. Acquired by the Ashmolean Museum in 1967, its provenance became questionable after a 2019 discovery. Following an investigation and a formal claim by India, the museum has agreed to its repatriation, highlighting a commitment to ethical collections.

title: Hornets vs Celtics injury report, player status and team stats (March 4)
content: The Charlotte Hornets, on a five-game winning streak, visit the Boston Celtics on March 4, 2026. Boston, despite missing Jayson Tatum due to injury, remains a top Eastern Conference team. Charlotte aims to improve its playoff standing with strong recent performances. This matchup promises significant implications for the conference standings as the season progresses.

title: Tariff may be hiked to 15% this week: Scott Bessent
content: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated President Trump's plan to raise universal tariffs to 15% could happen this week. While a temporary 10% levy is in place, authorities aim to reinstate previous tariffs within five months. Bessent also downplayed oil market risks from Middle East tensions, citing ample global supply and US support measures.

title: US says Spain ‘agreed to cooperate with US military’; foreign min denies White House claim
content: A diplomatic dispute has erupted between the US and Spain after the White House claimed Madrid agreed to cooperate with military operations against Iran. Spain's foreign minister categorically denied this, stating their position on base usage and the conflict remains unchanged. This follows Spain's earlier refusal to allow US forces to use its bases for strikes against Iran.

title: Finn Allen's record century powers New Zealand into T20 World Cup final
content: New Zealand stormed into the T20 World Cup final, crushing South Africa by nine wickets. Opener Finn Allen smashed a record-breaking 33-ball century, leading a dominant chase of 170.  Allen's explosive innings, supported by Tim Seifert's fifty, secured a comfortable victory with 7.1 overs to spare.

title: US orders non-emergency consular staff to leave Pakistan amid Middle East tensions
content: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions following US-Israeli strikes on Iran, the United States has ordered non-emergency staff and their families to leave consulates in Pakistan's Karachi and Lahore. Similar directives were issued for Saudi Arabia, Oman, and Cyprus, with American citizens advised to reconsider travel to these nations due to rising safety risks and disrupted flights.

title: West Asia tensions: 1,500 flights cancelled in 4 days, AI to add capacity on key long routes
content: Airlines are intensifying evacuation efforts with special flights as Middle East air travel disruptions continue for the fourth day. Indian carriers planned 58 flights on Wednesday, with IndiGo and Air India increasing capacity on long-haul routes. Airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi are handling limited repatriation flights, while services in Bahrain, Doha, and Kuwait remain suspended.

title: Apple MacBook Neo with A18 Pro chip, Liquid Retina display, and macOS Tahoe launched
content: Apple unveils its most budget-friendly laptop, the MacBook Neo, priced Rs 69,900. Featuring the powerful A18 Pro chip, a vibrant 13-inch display, and a sleek aluminum build, it promises impressive performance for everyday tasks and AI workloads.  This eco-conscious device, running macOS Tahoe with Apple Intelligence, is available for pre-order now.

title: Watch: Exact moment US strike hits Iranian ship, killing over 85
content: An Iranian warship, the IRIS Dena, was sunk in the Indian Ocean by a US submarine torpedo, resulting in at least 85 Iranian sailors killed. The Pentagon confirmed the strike as part of its campaign against Iran, with 32 sailors rescued and 148 still missing. Sri Lankan authorities recovered 87 bodies as search operations continue.

title: 'Huge danger': Broad flags Pandya as England’s biggest semifinal threat
content: Former England pacer Stuart Broad has issued a stark warning to England regarding Hardik Pandya ahead of their semifinal clash with India. Broad highlighted Pandya's ability to single-handedly change match momentum through his aggressive batting and crucial bowling. He emphasized that England must strategically target Pandya's batting order to neutralize his devastating finishing prowess.

title: Allen scripts history, becomes first-ever to achieve this T20 WC record
content: Finn Allen smashed a record 33-ball century — the fastest in T20 World Cup history — to lead New Zealand to a nine-wicket win over South Africa in the semi-final. Chasing 170, New Zealand finished in just 12.5 overs. Allen’s unbeaten 100 powered them into the final in dominant fashion.

title: Ships anchored off Gulf coasts after Strait of Hormuz blaze; Indian crew members express concern
content: A Chief Engineer from Hyderabad, currently aboard an anchored ship, noted that the vessel he is on has been stationary for days

title: Ahead in health, but a new set of challenges awaits Tamil Nadu
content: At the heart of Tamil Nadu’s successes in healthcare lies a dedicated public health system — a wide, well-distributed network of primary health centres.

title: ITF W35 women’s tennis tournament Kalaburagi: Ankita Raina and Zeel Desai advance to second round, but local favourite Soha Sadiq bows out
content: Local favourite Soha Sadiq thrilled the home crowd with a strong start against Singapore’s Eva Marie Desvignes, but could not advance to the next round

title: Two bodies recovered after fire breaks out in Delhi's Paharganj building
content: Twenty-five fire tenders were rushed to the spot after receiving the call, the Delhi Fire Service officer said

title: How U.S.-Israel attack on Iran could impact millions of Indian migrant workers
content: UAE, where Iranian missiles have landed targeting U.S. bases, is the second largest source of India’s inward remittances and also a large hub for Indian migrant workers engaged in blue-collar jobs

title: Transport operators in Maharashtra set for Statewide strike from Thursday; demand rollback of e-challan system
content: At the heart of the agitation is a slew of grievances, with the administration of e-challans emerging as the primary flashpoint

title: Nurses to continue strike in Thiruvananthapuram
content: UNA, which called the strike, represents the majority of nurses working in as many as 490 private hospitals, including clinics, in the State

title: Five women achievers honoured by Rajalakshmi Engineering College
content: The awards were given away by Thangam Meganathan, Chairperson, Rajalakshmi Institutions, in the presence of Tejshree Abhay Meganathan, Executive Director, Rajalakshmi Institutions

title: CM Stalin announces solatium to families of four persons killed in Gingee car accident
content: Four persons from Chennai were killed when the car they were travelling in fell into a roadside well at Alampoondi near Gingee on Tuesday

title: Sci-tech projects from 17 schools and 60 college groups showcased at Anna University on National Science Day
content: Quiz, drawing, and essay competitions were also organised where around 200 students from 30 schools participated

title: Ceramic industry in Gujarat's Morbi stares at shutdown as West Asia conflict disrupts gas supply
content: Petroleum and gas shipments coming from Gulf countries have been impacted, with consignments stuck at the Strait of Hormuz, which is under Iran's control

title: The SIR conundrum in West Bengal
content: While the Trinamool Congress under the leadership of Mamata Banerjee used SIR as an electoral opportunity and used the harassment of people to target the ECI and BJP led government at the Centre, the West Bengal BJP leadership appears to be disappointed with the outcome of the exercise as deletions in the electoral roll are far less than what they had wished for.

title: Excise detects rampant use of banned tobacco products by bus crew
content: The department booked over 30 cases under the Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products Act during an intensive drive held in district

title: Internal reservation: Dalit Ministers hold meetings to arrive at a consensus ahead of Karnataka Cabinet meeting on March 5
content: Ahead of the Cabinet meeting during which the internal reservation issue is scheduled to be discussed, Dalit Ministers held parleys to arrive at a possible broader consensus on the issue that has now driven a wedge among Scheduled Castes and left the Cabinet divided

title: DMK-Congress deal: the leaders who were instrumental in breaking the stalemate
content: Sustained efforts of Congress national president Mallikarjun Kharge, former Union Minister P. Chidambaram, DMK Parliamentary Party leader Kanimozhi, and Congress MP Karti P. Chidambaram helps the two parties reach an agreement on seat sharing

title: KGMCTA defers strike for a week following Kerala government assurances
content: Government agrees to disburse the pay revision arrears of 18 months immediately. Arrears of the remaining 39 months will be disbursed in accordance with legal advice

title: Kalamassery municipality mulls energy-efficient alternatives for street lights
content: The municipality has drafted a project to replace tube lights with LED lights in the first phase of the project expected to cost around ₹75 lakh

title: Applications open for IISER Aptitude Test 2026
content: Corrections in application forms will be carried out between April 16 and 18 and exam hall tickets will be released on May 24

title: CM Stalin inspects ongoing construction of elevated corridor on Anna Salai
content: The Chief Minister, along with the Minister for Public Works E.V. Velu and Minister for Health and Family Welfare Ma. Subramanian, inspected the 3.2 kilometre-long elevated road being constructed at a cost of ₹621 crore

title: Four workers faint after inhaling gas in effluent treatment plant in Ranipet
content: Initial inquiry revealed that the four workers had opened the tank, which is around 10 feet deep, to remove sludge

title: New Ranipet SP Sibin assumes charge
content: According to a press release, Mr. Sibin served as Superintendent of Police (SP) for Chengalpattu district

title: Textiles Minister inaugurates new Bypass Road in Ranipet
content: The new Bypass Road is funded under the Comprehensive Road Infrastructure Development Programme 2024-25

title: CBI launches probe into Puducherry counterfeit drugs case
content: The Central agency took over the case after investigation by local police revealed potential implications affecting public health, economic security, and national interest

title: Khazana Jewellery’s 268kg gold stock case: Madras High Court directs income tax settlement commission to reconsider settlement plea
content: Chief Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava and Justice G. Arul Murugan hold that the commission had rejected the plea in 2020 without proper consideration

title: SIR protests continue in West Bengal days after publication of the draft rolls
content: With the Assembly election due in a few months, the TMC may seek to keep the SIR pot boiling; Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee plans to sit in a dharna in Kolkata against the SIR from March 6

title: No possibility of merger of NCP factions any more, our position is clear, says Jayant Patil
content: Jayant Patil says reunification talks had been initiated at Ajit Pawar’s insistence and are no longer on the table.

title: Palestinian envoy to India slams ‘jungle law’ in Iran-U.S. conflict
content: Abu Shawesh has expressed deep concern over statements from major powers indicating that the window for negotiations has been closed

title: Congress will name Rajya Sabha candidate when filing nomination: Himachal CM
content: With a clear majority in the 68-member Himachal Pradesh Assembly, the Congress's victory should be a foregone conclusion, but for its bitter experience in the previous Rajya Sabha election

title: 180 flights cancelled amid growing West Asia conflict
content: Air India Express announced that its air services to Muscat will remain operational; Air India says it will deploy additional flights to Toronto, Frankfurt and Paris to cater to the high demand

title: Watch: Government’s silence on the Iran strike and Khamenei’s death draws opposition criticism
content: Indian opposition leaders have strongly criticised the Narendra Modi government for its silence after coordinated strikes by the United States and Israel that reportedly killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Parties including Rahul Gandhi and Sonia Gandhi have said that not condemning the attack or expressing condolences undermines India’s credibility and raises questions about its foreign policy, urging a parliamentary debate and clearer articulation of India’s stance.

title: Trade bodies flag impact of West Asia crisis on trade-driven Karnataka
content: Many Kannadigas have their business set-ups in Jebel Ali or in other trade free zones in West Asia; many travelled through Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar or Doha to reach leisure or business destinations in the U.S., or Europe

title: CM, DGP hold meeting with Amit Shah over talk of  impending surrender of top Maoist
content: Police deny such move and claim meeting was to brief the Union Home Minister about the naxalite movement and the requirements of the State police force

title: Foundation for ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel plant this month: CM
content: A shift from ‘Ease of Doing Business to Speed of Doing Business’ gave A.P. an edge over other States in attracting investments, says Chandrababu Naidu

title: Minister inaugurates rehabilitation colony for Ramayapatnam Port displaced families
content: Of the total cost of nearly ₹5,000 crore for the port, the State government has already spent ₹1,800 crore, and almost 77 per cent of the construction has been completed

title: Cochin Diocese’s post-SIR campaign seeks to ensure nobody loses right to vote
content: The diocese, comprising 40,750 families spread over four Assembly constituencies in two districts, has launched the drive to identify those who remain out of the electoral rolls even after the recently concluded Special Intensive Revision of voters’ list

title: Social activist flags frequent fires in Magadi taluk
content: Shabnam Hashmi, who runs Ajji’s Learning Centre, a non-profit education trust located in Jyothipalya village, said that since February 24, there have been three instances of forest fires.

title: Karnataka’s HPV vaccination plan revived under Centre’s national rollout
content: The programme announced in Karnataka earlier could not take off as planned due to delays in vaccine procurement

title: Before announcing W.B. poll dates, EC members to assess ground situation in the State from March 8
content: Full Commission to meet State poll, law enforcement officials, political parties; to check logistics, identify high tension areas for extra security deployment; plans for CCTV cameras outside poll booths

title: 90% data integration for Family Benefit Management System likely by April 30
content: FBMS is envisioned as a centralised digital platform to ensure transparent, targeted and seamless delivery of welfare benefits to eligible families across Andhra Pradesh

title: RCB matches at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium: Final clearance yet to come
content: The Karnataka State Cricket Association is required to comply with 17 major suggestions drafted by the expert committee to get final clearance; it has only complied with two so far

title: Gulf conflict: A.P. trying to evacuate stranded people in three days
content: Communication with Telugu people currently in Iran was yet to be fully established, and efforts were under way to reach out to them through diplomatic and community channels, says Minister Kondapalli Srinivas

title: P.K. Sasi to quit CPI(M), to contest in Ottapalam as Independent
content: While CPI(M) leaders insist that his exit will not dent the party’s prospects, UDF hopes to leverage his influence

title: Seat-sharing talks progressed well, LDF to announce candidates soon: Ramakrishnan
content: LDF convener says  organisational work for ensuring front’s  victory in the elections is ongoing, adding the response of the public during house visits has been encouraging

title: West Asia crisis: Kerala launches counselling support to expats, families back home
content: Expatriate Keralites and their family members can access 24X7 the services of counsellors under ‘Tele-Manas.’ Anxiety triggered by exaggerated portrayal of the crisis by a section of the media is causing greater distress to expatriates and their families, says NoRKA-Roots CEO

title: SSLC, Plus One exams begin on Thursday
content: 4.18 lakh students have registered for SSLC examinations. 4.11 lakh students will appear for the Plus One higher secondary examinations and 3.41 lakh students for the Plus One improvement examinations

title: Fresh concern after Yathindra says stadium will come up at KSIC premises in T. Narasipura
content: The MLC says the officials, who had explored various sites, had identified the KSIC factory land as the most suitable site

title: It is a riot of colours in Dharwad
content: Thousands of people of all age groups come out in large numbers to celebrate Holi festival despite a blazing Sun

title: Parents body opposes government move to scrap APHERMC
content: As the State government plans to replace APHERMC with Fee Regulatory Committee, the PAAP members allege that it will allow the managements of private institutions to fleece parents

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: Tightrope walk for Chief Minister Siddaramaiah
content: With annual spending for guarantees exceeding ₹50,000 crore, Siddaramaiah may appeal to relatively well-off families to voluntarily give up certain benefits

title: Governor signs Malayalam Language Bill, making Malayalam Kerala’s official language
content: New law mandates use of Malayalam in government administration, and extends to semi-government bodies, public sector undertakings and cooperative institutions

title: Twin joys from students’ farming efforts in Thrissur
content: NSS members of Govt. HSS, Marathamkode, laboured on a 35-cent plot and harvested 280 kg. The students then donated the profit from the harvest for the heart surgery of a baby

title: Holtz, college football coaching great, dies at 89
content: Lou Holtz, the quick-witted college football coach who led Notre Dame to a national championship in 1988 and burnished his reputation as a master at rebuilding programs, has died at the age of 89.

title: Browns' Garrett gets 9th speeding ticket since '17
content: Browns star Myles Garrett was issued a citation for speeding -- his ninth since entering the NFL in 2017 -- after he was clocked going 94 mph in a 70 mph zone.

title: S. Alabama-CCU brawl injures ref, sees 8 ejected
content: A brawl between South Alabama and Coastal Carolina in the women's Sun Belt Tournament on Wednesday left eight players ejected and knocked a referee to the ground where she required medical attention.

title: Rory: 'A shame' Rahm rejected DP World Tour deal
content: Rory McIlroy says it's "a shame" that Jon Rahm is the only LIV Golf player unwilling to accept the terms of a DP World Tour deal to eliminate future fines.

title: Man pleads guilty in athlete home burglaries case
content: Alexander Esteban Huaiquil Chavez, a member of a theft group comprising Chilean nationals that burglarized the homes of athletes across the United States in 2024, pleaded guilty to interstate transportation of stolen property on Wednesday.

title: Brighton boss slams Arsenal tactics, tells PL to act
content: Brighton boss Fabian Hürzeler has launched a scathing attack on Arsenal's time-wasting tactics by calling on the Premier League to intervene.

title: Rodgers still undecided despite McCarthy's hire
content: Veteran QB Aaron Rodgers says he remains undecided on his playing status for next season despite the Steelers hiring Mike McCarthy, with whom he won a Super Bowl in Green Bay.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Ohio State, Miami create separation with key wins
content: The Buckeyes and Canes solidified their standings on the right side of the respective Big Ten and ACC bubbles.

title: Process over perfection: How UConn prepared to chase its third title in four years
content: The pressure of expectation derailed the Huskies' bid for a three-peat. Perspective has them back in contention.

title: Ready for NFL free agency? We have the latest buzz on active teams, player-team fits and more
content: Here's what you need to know about 2026 free agency. We picked out everything from players who might break the bank to sleepers to watch.

title: Trade grades: Did the Rams give up too much to acquire Trent McDuffie?
content: We're handing out report cards on offseason moves, including the Chiefs sending Trent McDuffie to the Rams.

title: Emma Hayes' USWNT vision for 2027 World Cup is becoming clear
content: After so much experimentation, how the USWNT will look under Emma Hayes at the 2027 Women's World Cup is becoming evident.

title: MLB Rank 2026: Ranking baseball's top 100 players
content: From star sluggers like Ohtani and Judge to top pitchers like Skubal, we rank -- and make predictions for -- the best players in baseball right now.

title: 'He wants a Cy Young': With his arm at full strength, Shohei Ohtani is a man on a mission
content: Dodgers teammates say MLB's most singular star has set his sights on another history-making achievement.

title: Women's Bracketology: Welcome to the top 16, Minnesota
content: Sunday's reveal put a new team in the top 16. The start of Champ Week will bring more answers as the bracket comes into focus.

title: Arsenal don't need to make friends to win the Premier League title
content: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler had strong words for Arsenal's game management, but the Gunners now control their own destiny in the title race.

title: Have two potential Manchester City players ended the club's title hopes?
content: Morgan Gibbs-White and Elliot Anderson might have just dented Man City's chances of winning the league.

title: 2026 World Cup squads: Predicting starting XIs for England, Spain, France, more
content: With under 100 days to go until the 2026 World Cup, how do all the major contenders -- and the three co-hosts -- look in terms of squads and starters? We break down how things stand as the countdown heats up.

title: How many future Hall of Famers are playing in the 2026 WBC?
content: From the locks to the long shots to those in between, we count Cooperstown candidates in this year's World Baseball Classic.

title: Chauncey Billups back in court: Why the Blazers coach is facing decades in prison
content: After a Hall of Fame NBA career, here's what we know about Billups' connections to Mafia-affiliated poker games and illegal gambling.

title: Vincent Trocheck? Aggressive Sabres? What we're hearing days ahead of the NHL trade deadline
content: With the trade deadline set for Friday, it has been fairly quiet this week. But all it takes is one big deal to get things moving.

title: 'I think she's coming back': Djokovic buzzes about Serena Williams' possible return
content: Novak Djokovic said he thought Wimbledon would be a great potential site for Serena Williams to officially make her return.

title: Inside a surprise dance performance from UCLA women's basketball stars
content: Lauren Betts, Gabriela Jaquez and Charlisse Leger-Walker high kicked their way onto the UCLA dance team.

title: How to bet the Arnold Palmer Invitational: Højgaard, Lowry positioned well at Bay Hill
content: The next stop on the PGA Tour season brings us to Bay Hill Club and Lodge for the Arnold Palmer Invitational, where length off the tee and controlled iron play is at a premium.

title: The craziest F1 start ever? Why Australian Grand Prix might see chaos from the start
content: Grand prix starts have always held the potential for chaos, but this year there's almost guaranteed drama when the lights go out in Australia on Sunday. ESPN explains why.

title: Has Aston Martin's F1 super team turned into a disaster team?
content: Fernando Alonso described Martin as the "team of the future" -- but it heads to Australia in disarray.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United, Chelsea, Liverpool eye USMNT star
content: Manchester United, Chelsea, and Liverpool are monitoring the situation of Bournemouth and USMNT midfielder Tyler Adams. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: The in-your-face history of the BMF belt, as told by the man who inspired it
content: Max Holloway and Charles Oliveira can thank Nate Diaz for the chance to add BMF to their UFC legacies.

title: Divisional rankings: Navarrete keeps top spot with dominant win over Nunez
content: Emanuel Navarette dominated Eduardo Nunez to keep his top spot at 130 pounds. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: 'These fights have to happen': Opetaia chasing undisputed status with Zuffa debut
content: Australia's Jai Opetaia will make his Zuffa Boxing debut against Brandon Glanton in Las Vegas this weekend.

title: Gold, Silver Rise In International Markets, Check Price In Your City
content: According to the Bullions website, gold in India is currently quoted at Rs 1,62,100 per 10 grams, while silver stands at Rs 2,66,360 per kg.

title: Stock Market Live: Sensex, Nifty Open In Green After Wall Street Gains
content: Stock Market LIVE Updates: Sensex was at 79,443, up 327 points or 0.41%. Nifty rallied 100 points at the open.

title: Gen Z vs Old Guard: Nepal Poll Pits Balendra Shah Against Veteran Leaders
content: Nepal kicks off its first general election since a Gen Z-led uprising that ousted the former government last year, with a new crop of young candidates emerging to challenge the old guard's grip on power in the Himalayan nation.

title: Telangana Man Injured In Attack Near Abu Dhabi Airport, Family Seeks Help
content: Tension in the Gulf region has left a Telangana family worried after a man from the Rajanna Sircilla district was reportedly injured in an attack near Abu Dhabi International Airport.

title: Nepal Elections 2026 Live Updates: "My Duty Is Completed," Says Interim PM Sushila Karki After Voting
content: Nearly 19 million of Nepal's 30 million people are eligible to vote to pick a 275-member legislature.

title: "Baseless": India Rejects Report Claiming US Using Its Ports For Iran Strikes
content: A video of former US Army Colonel Douglas Macgregor has gone viral, in which he suggested that the US was using Indian naval bases to attack Iran.

title: Iris Dena Highlights: "Single Mark 48 Torpedo" Used To Sink Iranian Warship: US
content: IRIS Dena was returning from India after participating in the 2026 International Fleet Review in Visakhapatnam when it was reportedly attacked and later sank off the southern coast of Sri Lanka.

title: "15 Out Of 10": Trump Rates US War Performance Against Iran
content: President Donald Trump on Wednesday rated the US performance in the war with Iran as a 15 on a scale of 10, saying the Islamic republic's leaders were rapidly being killed, and vowing to push on.

title: "Iran Will Fight Until Last Moment": Geopolitical Strategist To NDTV On Ongoing War
content: The Geopolitical Strategist expressed concern over risks if Iran starts attacking ships once the US Navy starts escorting ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Pentagon Identifies 6 US Soldiers Killed In Kuwait Drone Strike
content: The last two names of the six US soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack were released Wednesday by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa.

title: Nagpur Woman Pours Hot Water On Grandson, 4, For Spraying Colour On Her
content: Enraged over her four-year-old grandson spraying paint on her while playing, his grandmother allegedly poured boiling water on the child, resulting in scalding injuries, in Koradi area of Nagpur district, police said on Wednesday.

title: A Look Back At The 2015 Nepal Earthquake When 9,000 People Died
content: Over a decade ago, a devastating earthquake in Nepal killed around 9,000 people and left hundreds of thousands homeless.

title: Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Launches Missile Barrage At Israel As Attacks In Middle East Commence For 6th Day
content: Israel-Iran War live updates: Iran launched missiles at Israel as aerial attacks in the Middle East commenced for a sixth day after an American submarine sank an Iranian warship and Iran threatened destruction of military

title: Nepal Votes To Elect New Government Today, First Since Gen-Z Protests
content: Nepal is voting today - a first since last year's Gen Z protests that toppled the government and led to the death of at least 77 people. The election will replace the interim government of Sushila Karki that has been leading the Himalayan country.

title: Apple Launches Entry-Level MacBook Neo Starting Rs 69,900. Details Inside
content: Apple CEO Tim Cook promised a "big week" of product announcements that will see the introduction of a new budget-friendly iPhone trim, an entry-level MacBook tier, updated iPad Air models, refreshed monitors and higher-end chipsets.

title: Iran War "Could Last 8 Weeks", Pete Hegseth Says US "Can't Stop Everything"
content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth acknowledged on Wednesday that some Iranian air attacks may still hit their targets even as he asserted that US military superiority is quickly giving it control of the Islamic Republic's airspace.

title: BJP Begins Final Candidate Screening For Assam Assembly Polls: Himanta Sarma
content: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma on Wednesday ceremonially distributed appointment letters to newly selected Assam Civil Service (ACS) officers and other allied service candidates at the Damodardeva International Auditorium in Panjabari, Guwahati.

title: White House Says Trump Using Preventative Skin Treatment For Neck Rash
content: US President Donald Trump is using a preventative treatment for a red rash on his neck, according to his physician, but the White House declined on Wednesday to share further details about the condition.

title: Amazon Cuts More Jobs; This Time In Robotics Unit
content: Amazon on Tuesday confirmed it laid off staff across its robotics unit, with at least 100 white-collar jobs affected, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

title: 💍 Tennis star Sabalenka announces engagement
content: INDIAN WELLS, CA -- Top-ranked tennis player  Aryna Sabalenka  has announced her engagement to Brazilian businessman Georgios Frangulis. Sabalenka posted a video of the proposal on Instagram, accompanied by the words "You & me, forever," along with a ring and heart emoji. A post shared by Aryna Sabalenka (@arynasabalenka) The news quickly drew congratulations from fellow tennis players, including  Carlos Alcaraz ,  Novak Djokovic ,  Ben Shelton ,  Caroline Wozniacki ,  Coco Gauff  and  Amanda Anisimova , who is a possible opponent for Sabalenka in the quarterfinals at the forthcoming Indian Wells tournament in the Southern California desert. U.S. women's rugby player Ilona Maher also sent her congratulations. The tournament, which opens on Wednesday, will be Sabalenka's first since she reached the Australian Open final, where she lost to  Elena Rybakina  6-4, 4-6, 6-4 on Jan. 31.
